Current Cohort.name: Jeenu Pawar
Current Cohort.focus: Empowering Phase - Pardhi communities through education
Current Cohort.about: Education facilities in small villages were extremely restricted. The main impediment to rural education is uneducated parents, poor financial situation, lack of participation in education, and ignorance about educational benefits. Children and adolescents are the most affected by this condition. Shri Rudra Welfare Foundation, Nagpur creates the center in rural village which is known as "Anandghar - A Place of Joy". A spot where children will spend their time happily doing fun things and learning life skills. They openly express their emotions and thoughts to the elderly without hesitation. They want them to be happy and learning during their child time. They studied the villages and chose the most affected areas for this reason. They are launching a two-hour regular program to teach enjoyable learning education to children of various ages. They use educational fun games, social media, activity-based teaching, and other methods to achieve this goal. We provide nutritious food on a regular basis for their overall health, such as fruits, nuts, chikki, dietary food, and so on. They link the youth through this center and have a good understanding of their issues by created a youth katta for this reason, where They provide them with career advice and solve their other problems. This aided them in launching a youth-oriented career initiative.

Current Cohort.name: Sheshraj Shisodiya
Current Cohort.focus: Empowering Phase - Pardhi communities through education
Current Cohort.about: Lahar is a youth-led collective from the Pardhi community that works to strengthen access to education and address identity-related challenges. Through creative and participatory methods—such as theatre, sports, libraries, and community meetings—Lahar engages children and youth to spark awareness and interest in education. The collective also tackles critical issues including child marriage, access to panchayat and judicial rights, and mental health. Lahar believes that true empowerment comes when the community itself recognizes its identity and stands up for its rights.

Current Cohort.name: Akram
Current Cohort.focus: Nurturing values through art and dialogue
Current Cohort.about: Kala Samvad Foundation works to foster values and drive positive social change through art and dialogue. Believing that every child and young person carries unique creativity, the foundation creates open and creative spaces—through dance, music, theatre, literature, and other art forms—where children and youth can express themselves and build confidence. Its workshops, cultural events, and dialogue programs aim to strengthen values of love, peace, equality, and fraternity, while connecting art with everyday life to inspire social awareness and transformation.

Current Cohort.name: Ohab Ansari
Current Cohort.focus: Taking education and dignity to every doorstep of Mir, Banjara & Fakir Community
Current Cohort.about:
Since 2018, Samavesh is working with four major nomadic tribes—Mir, Fakir, Banjara, and Od—living in temporary settlements across villages and slums in Gujarat. These communities face deep challenges, relying on begging and daily wage labor, with little access to education, healthcare, or basic services.

The initiative engages 312 families across six villages and three towns in Mehsana, Sabarkantha, and Banaskantha districts. Its focus is on expanding access to education, building awareness of rights, and fostering dignity and social inclusion for nomadic communities. They believe that true inclusion and transformation can only be achieved when education and dignity reach the most marginalized.

Current Cohort.name: Priti & Anil
Current Cohort.focus: Empowering nomadic lives through identity and education
Current Cohort.about: Registered in 2013, Samaj Uttan Seva Samiti works with Denotified and Nomadic Tribes (DNTs and NTs), among the most marginalized communities deprived of stable housing, education, and basic services. Its core focus is connecting children to mainstream education while also helping families secure legal identity documents and access government welfare schemes. Emerging from within the community itself, the organization fosters youth leadership—among both women and men—so they can advocate for their rights and drive change from within.

Current Cohort.name: Mehruddin
Current Cohort.focus: Smiles of learning, earning, and living well.
Current Cohort.about:
Project Smile works to ensure that children from economically disadvantaged families are not deprived of education by providing free learning support and study materials. Alongside this, the project empowers educated yet unemployed youth through skill development programs—run in collaboration with partners like Sanjhe Sapne and School for Programming—that build employable skills and connect them to job opportunities. In the area of health, Project Smile facilitates affordable diagnostic services and raises awareness about government welfare schemes, helping families access their rightful entitlements.

Its vision is simple yet powerful: every child educated, every youth employed, and every family healthy and aware.

Current Cohort.name: Dashrath
Current Cohort.focus: Nurturing dreams of Katkari children, one learning space at a time
Current Cohort.about: Sanvedan Kendra works with the Katkari nomadic tribe in Goa, one of the most marginalized communities in the region. The organization focuses on ensuring that Katkari children not only attend school but also thrive—through daily remedial classes in settlements, individualized support within schools, and residential learning camps that offer safe, inspiring spaces for growth. These efforts are helping improve learning outcomes, build confidence, and nurture aspirations—laying the foundation for lasting community transformation.

Current Cohort.name: Phoolchand
Current Cohort.focus: Lighting up dreams of tribal girls in Chhattisgarh.
Current Cohort.about: JUGNI works with tribal adolescent girls (ages 13–18) in remote areas of Chhattisgarh, where distance, household responsibilities, and gender inequality often push them out of school. Through its learning center—a vibrant space that serves as both a library and a hub for tailored programs—JUGNI helps girls reconnect with education while building awareness of their rights, leadership, and life skills. The initiative empowers every girl to pursue her studies, nurture her dreams, and step forward with confidence and dignity.

Current Cohort.name: Nirmala Jhala
Current Cohort.focus: Education that travels with the pastoralists
Current Cohort.about: Nirmala works with the Bharwad pastoralist families in the Jalgaon district of Maharashtra—communities displaced from Gujarat at the time of Independence and still living lives of constant movement. For them, even basic needs like food, shelter, and education are daily struggles. Through her initiative, Nirmala strives to bring education into their everyday lives—not just as a means of learning, but as a pathway to dignity, stability, and hope for a better future.

2024-25.name: Mukund Kale
2024-25.org: Spark Social Education and Welfare Society
2024-25.focus: Igniting Change and Community Action in Vadar Community
2024-25.about:
Established in 2016, Spark Foundation has been working to support underprivileged and aspiring students in rural areas by providing clothes, school uniforms, books, notebooks, and bags. Beyond education, the organization undertakes tree plantation drives on Gram Panchayat land, maintaining them consistently for two to three years to promote environmental sustainability.

During periods of drought, Spark addressed the drinking water shortage in a local school by collaboratively installing a borewell with the help of villagers—an example of their strong participatory approach.

The organization also focuses on student motivation, career guidance, and higher education support, organizing regular discussions, showing motivational videos, and assisting students with college admissions and financial aid in cities like Pune and Mumbai. Recently, Spark has initiated a learning space for children from the Vadar community, emphasizing education and environmental awareness to foster holistic growth and equal opportunities.

Web: https://www.linkedin.com/company/spark-social-education-and-welfare-society/posts/?feedView=all

Web Status : Functional but not consistent in efforts

2024-25.name: Mirtunjay Bharadwaj
2024-25.focus: Connecting children from slums and marginalized communities to mainstream society
2024-25.about:
Ramaamani Bhagvatsing Foundation, based in Begusarai, Bihar, works with community participation across sectors such as education, environment, road safety, sports, and healthcare. Its primary focus is on connecting children from slums and marginalized communities to mainstream society through a community-supported school, ensuring access to education and equal opportunities for growth.

Web: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mirtunjay-02725a2b8/

Web Status : Functional but not consistent in efforts

2024-25.name: Sanchit
2024-25.focus: Building bridges between Urdu and Marathi
2024-25.about:
The Shiksha Initiative focuses on enhancing early literacy by building the capacity of Anganwadi workers in Chhattisgarh to adopt an emergent literacy approach for teaching scripts to young children. The initiative develops contextual teaching and learning materials—including shared reading resources and read-aloud books—to make learning engaging and effective. By strengthening foundational literacy at the grassroots, the initiative aims to create a replicable model adaptable to Anganwadis across diverse regions of India.

Web: https://www.linkedin.com/in/sanchit-patne-3502ba185/?originalSubdomain=in

Web Status : NOT FUNCTIONAL - Working with different orgs

2024-25.name: Sarswati & Deepak
2024-25.about:
The STEP Foundation works for social change across urban, rural, and tribal areas of Nashik district, addressing issues related to education, health, environment, women's empowerment, youth, and farmers. The organization has conducted health awareness programs through street plays in over 70 government ashram schools, organized competitions to promote social values and artistic expression, and held workshops on waste management and constitutional awareness. STEP also runs 'Nai Talim' sessions to foster constitutional and human values among children and operates a vocational training center for women and a community library for youth, women, and children.

Web: https://www.linkedin.com/in/step-foundation-nashik-a87175320/

Web Status : Functional and consistent

2024-25.name: Vijay Kumar Paswan
2024-25.focus: Lighting up learning in Musahar communities
2024-25.about:
Aatmvishwas Foundation has been working in the areas of education and youth development for the past two years. The organization runs a Community Learning Center for children from the Musahar community, providing both basic and social education. Alongside this, Aatmvishwas works with youth to build self-confidence, create awareness of new opportunities, and nurture social leadership qualities.

Web: https://aatmvishwasfoundation.in/

Web Status : Functional and consistent

2024-25.name: Smita & Khushashri
2024-25.focus: From selling on Goa's beaches to dreaming beyond the waves
2024-25.about:
The organization works with children of laborers and migrant workers in Mapusa, Goa, who often drop out of schools in their home states due to frequent migration. Once out of school, returning becomes difficult because of financial constraints, language barriers, and social discrimination.
To address this, the organization creates child-friendly and inclusive learning spaces that keep children engaged through joyful, activity-based learning. These spaces help children rebuild confidence, nurture curiosity, and gradually reintegrate into the formal education system — giving them a fair chance at a brighter future.

Web Status : Not functional currently as works with migrant community

2024-25.name: Shubhangi & Vikas
2024-25.org: Mahamanav Baba Amte Bahuuddeshiya Samajik Vikas Seva Sanstha
2024-25.focus: From struggle to self-reliance, with compassion
2024-25.about:
Mahamanav Baba Amte Samajik Vikas Seva Sanstha, established in 2008 in Shrigonda, Ahmednagar, is a non-profit organization committed to community upliftment and social progress. Inspired by the values of transformative social leaders, the organization works to break the cycle of poverty by providing holistic support and quality education to marginalized children, especially from nomadic tribes.
By promoting inclusivity, skill development, and emotional well-being, the organization nurtures environments where every child can learn and grow. It also serves as a catalyst for community engagement—fostering empathy, awareness, and collective responsibility toward building a more compassionate society.

Web : https://aatmvishwasfoundation.in/

Web Status : Not functional currently as works with migrant community

2023-24.name: Laxmi Rathore
2023-24.focus: Empowering Phase - Pardhi communities through education
2023-24.about: Laxmi Rathore is a dedicated community leader focused on the Pardhi community in Bhopal. Her work through Majal Foundation creates safe learning spaces where children can thrive and families are supported in their educational journey.

2023-24.name: Shalini Rajawat
2023-24.about: Shalini Rajawat works tirelessly to ensure that nomadic children in Gwalior have access to formal education. She advocates for their rights and works with local authorities to simplify admission processes.

2023-24.name: Pradeep Kumar
2023-24.focus: Art as a medium for community learning
2023-24.about: Pradeep Kumar uses creative arts to engage children from marginalized backgrounds. His workshops foster creativity and critical thinking, making learning an enjoyable experience for the community.

2023-24.name: Sushant Mishra
2023-24.about: Sushant Mishra leads educational initiatives in rural Rewa, focusing on holistic growth. His centers provide not just academic support but also life skills training for young adolescents.

2023-24.name: Pratibha Singh
2023-24.about: Pratibha Singh runs mobile learning centers for children living on the streets of Varanasi. Her mission is to bring the classroom to them and eventually transition them into formal schools.

2023-24.name: Sumit Yadav
2023-24.about: Sumit Yadav works at the intersection of parent livelihood and child education. By supporting families' economic stability, he ensures that children can remain in center-based learning environments.

2022-23.name: Rohan Deshmukh
2022-23.about: Rohan Deshmukh has spent the last year working with rural schools to improve teaching quality and student engagement in the Amravati district.

2022-23.name: Sneha Patil
2022-23.about: Sneha Patil focuses on improving hygiene awareness alongside education in Pune's underprivileged urban areas.

2022-23.name: Vikram Singh
2022-23.focus: Revitalizing traditional learning for modern contexts
2022-23.about: Vikram Singh works with traditional artisan communities to ensure their children receive a balanced education that respects their heritage.

2022-23.name: Kavita Reddy
2022-23.about: Kavita Reddy runs digital literacy bootcamps for girls in Hyderabad, preparing them for future career opportunities.

2022-23.name: Amit Sharma
2022-23.about: Amit Sharma's focus is on the critical early years of learning, establishing pre-schools that serve low-income families.

2021-22.name: Siddharth Jain
2021-22.about: Siddharth Jain implemented technology solutions to help children in Mumbai's outskirts continue learning during challenging times.

2021-22.name: Meera Iyer
2021-22.about: Meera Iyer's work ensures that children with diverse learning needs are included in mainstream community learning centers.

2021-22.name: Rahul Bose
2021-22.about: Rahul Bose uses theatre and music as tools for engagement and education with street children in Kolkata.

2021-22.name: Divya Gupta
2021-22.about: Divya Gupta bridges the gap between basic education and vocational skills for adolescents in Lucknow.

2021-22.name: Suresh Prabhu
2021-22.about: Suresh Prabhu integrates environmental awareness into the local curriculum for children from coastal communities.

2020-21.name: Maya Venkat
2020-21.about: Maya Venkat pioneered community-led literacy programs that empowered local volunteers to teach children in their own neighborhoods.
